Treatment Arm.reasoning: The abstract on page 1 states: "The phase 3 TALAPRO-2 study investigated combining the poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibitor talazoparib with enzalutamide versus enzalutamide alone as first-line treatment of mCRPC. Patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to talazoparib or placebo, plus enzalutamide." Table 1 on page 2 also lists the two arms as "Talazoparib + enzalutamide" and "Placebo + enzalutamide".

Type of Therapy.reasoning: The abstract on page 1 mentions "combining the poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibitor talazoparib with enzalutamide versus enzalutamide alone", indicating a combination of therapies.

Type of Combination Therapy.value: PARP inhibitor + Androgen Receptor Pathway Inhibitor (ARPI)
Type of Combination Therapy.reasoning: The abstract on page 1 identifies talazoparib as a "poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibitor" and enzalutamide as an "androgen receptor inhibitor". The Methods section on page 9 refers to enzalutamide as an 'androgen receptor pathway inhibitor'.

Type of Agents.reasoning: The abstract on page 1 and Table 1 on page 2 clearly state the agents used in the treatment arms: "talazoparib with enzalutamide versus enzalutamide alone" (where enzalutamide alone implies enzalutamide + placebo).

Prior Therapy in Any Setting.value: Prior second-generation androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (Abiraterone or Orteronel); Prior taxane-based chemotherapy (Docetaxel)
Prior Therapy in Any Setting.reasoning: Table 1 on page 2 lists "Prior treatment with a second-generation androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (Abiraterone, Orteronel)" and "Prior taxane-based chemotherapy" as baseline characteristics. The Methods section on page 9 also states, "Randomization was stratified by prior second-generation androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (abiraterone or orteronel) or docetaxel (yes/no)."

Prior Therapy in M0CRPC Setting.reasoning: The document does not specifically mention prior therapy in the M0CRPC setting. Prior therapies are discussed in the context of castration-sensitive disease or general prior treatment.

Prior Therapy in mCSPC Setting.reasoning: The Methods section on page 9 states, "Prior docetaxel and abiraterone or orteronel in the castration-sensitive setting were allowed." Page 3 also mentions "prior abiraterone or orteronel (a CYP17 inhibitor) or docetaxel for castration-sensitive disease". Page 6 further confirms "Over one-third of the HRR-deficient population in TALAPRO-2 had received prior docetaxel or abiraterone for castration-sensitive disease". Castration-sensitive disease is equivalent to mCSPC in this context.

Prior Therapy in mCRPC Setting.reasoning: The study is for "first-line treatment of mCRPC" as stated in the abstract on page 1, implying that patients had not received prior therapy specifically for mCRPC in this trial.
